column3: DSL provides broadband service over telephone lines.
column4:
Memory Trick

“Subscriber line” connects the acronym to the provider's telephone-line infrastructure.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.18; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze DSL Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: A WISP supplies terrestrial internet access over a wireless link.
column4:
Exam Trap

The word “wireless” does not make WISP synonymous with satellite or cellular service.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.19; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze WISP Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column2: DOCSIS stands for {{c1::Data Over Cable Service Interface Specification}}.
column3: DOCSIS is associated with data service over cable-provider infrastructure.
column4:
Exam Trap

Remember the cable-service association; version-specific features are not required for this card.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.18; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze DOCSIS Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: A LAN covers a limited local area such as a building or nearby group of buildings.
column4:
Memory Trick

“Local” is relative, but the network remains limited compared with a WAN.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.19; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze LAN Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: A WAN commonly connects LANs across substantial geographic distance.
column4:
Exam Trap

WAN describes geographic scope, not one required provider technology.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.19; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze WAN Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: A PAN connects devices over a short range around one person.
column4:
Exam Trap

PAN is about personal scope; Bluetooth, infrared, or NFC may provide the link.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.19; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze PAN Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: A MAN spans a city or metropolitan region.
column4:
Memory Trick

The geographic scale generally falls between a LAN and a broad WAN.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.19; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze MAN Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: A SAN supplies networked block-level storage to systems.
column4:
Exam Trap

Do not confuse SAN with a normal shared-folder service, which provides file-level access.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.19; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze SAN Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: A WLAN commonly uses 802.11 within a limited local area.
column4:
Exam Trap

WLAN identifies the local wireless network, not the internet connection feeding it.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.19; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.7
column9: A+::220-1201::2.7 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::InternetConnectionandNetworkTypes Cloze WLAN Acronym Source::Messer-v170
